Data Pipeline Engineering.aiRubric: Check ETL concepts, pandas logic, Airflow DAG creation, API requests, and Kafka producer understanding.

lessons.title: Introduction to Data Pipelines & ETL Concepts
lessons.theory:
## ETL
Extract, Transform, Load. It is the core process of moving and cleaning data.
```text
Raw Data -> [Extract] -> [Transform] -> [Load] -> Data Warehouse
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: ETL Phases
Assign the string `'Transform'` to the variable describing the phase where data is cleaned.

lessons.title: Building ETL Pipelines with Pandas & Python
lessons.theory:
## Pandas ETL
Extract from CSV, filter missing data, and load to a new file.
```python
df = pd.read_csv('data.csv')
df.dropna(inplace=True)
df.to_csv('clean.csv')
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Drop Nulls
Call the `dropna` method on the dataframe `df`.
lessons.starterCode:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv('data.csv')
df.___()
lessons.solution:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv('data.csv')
df.dropna()

lessons.title: Workflow Automation & Task Orchestration with Apache Airflow
lessons.theory:
## Airflow DAGs
Directed Acyclic Graphs define task dependencies in Python.
```python
task1 = PythonOperator(task_id='t1', python_callable=func1)
task1 >> task2
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: DAG Dependency
Use the `>>` bitshift operator to make `task2` run after `task1`.

lessons.title: Ingesting Data from REST APIs & Webhooks
lessons.theory:
## Ingestion
Pulling JSON data from an endpoint and extracting the relevant payload.
```python
data = requests.get('api').json()['payload']
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Extract JSON Payload
Extract the `'results'` key from the `.json()` response.
lessons.starterCode:
import requests
res = requests.get('https://api.example.com')
data = res.___()['___']
lessons.solution:
import requests
res = requests.get('https://api.example.com')
data = res.json()['results']

lessons.title: Stream Processing Basics with Apache Kafka
lessons.theory:
## Kafka
A distributed event streaming platform. Producers send events to Topics.
```python
producer.send('my-topic', value=b'event_data')
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Kafka Topic
Send the byte-string `b'login'` to the topic `'user-events'`.
lessons.rubric: Producer sends correct value to correct topic.

Feature Stores & Scale.aiRubric: Check Data Warehouse concepts, feature store utility, PySpark commands, vector DB knowledge, and data validation techniques.

lessons.title: Introduction to Data Warehouses (BigQuery/Snowflake)
lessons.theory:
## Data Warehousing
Optimized for OLAP (Online Analytical Processing) workloads, using columnar storage.
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_events
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Workload Type
Assign `'OLAP'` to the variable describing data warehouse workloads.

lessons.title: Building & Managing Feature Stores for Machine Learning
lessons.theory:
## Feature Stores
A centralized vault for storing, retrieving, and sharing curated ML features to prevent training-serving skew.
```python
fs.get_online_features(features=['user_age'])
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Feature Store Goal
Assign the string `'Training-Serving Skew'` to the variable representing the main problem Feature Stores prevent.

lessons.title: Managing Big Data with PySpark
lessons.theory:
## PySpark
Spark distributes processing across clusters. Data is stored in Resilient Distributed Datasets (RDDs) or DataFrames.
```python
df = spark.read.json("logs.json")
df.filter(df.age > 21).show()
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Spark Filter
Use the `filter` method on `df` to keep only rows where `df.status == 'active'`.

lessons.title: Scaling Production Vector Databases for AI Systems
lessons.theory:
## Vector DBs
Databases like Pinecone or Milvus optimized for high-dimensional semantic similarity search.
```python
index.upsert(vectors=[("id1", [0.1, 0.2, 0.3])])
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Upsert Vector
Call the `upsert` method to insert vector `[0.5, 0.5]` under the ID `'doc_2'`.
lessons.rubric: upsert method used with vectors list.

lessons.title: Automated Data Quality Testing & Validation
lessons.theory:
## Data Validation
Tools like Great Expectations ensure incoming data matches the expected schema.
```python
validator.expect_column_values_to_not_be_null("age")
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Expectation
Write the method call to expect that column `'email'` should not be null.

LLMOps & Production.aiRubric: Check ML CI/CD concepts, RAG evaluation metrics, deployment logic, production monitoring, and distributed compute scaling.

lessons.title: CI/CD Pipelines for ML/AI Models (GitHub Actions + DVC)
lessons.theory:
## DVC
Data Version Control handles tracking large models/datasets while Git tracks code.
```bash
dvc add data/dataset.csv
git commit -m "Update data"
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: DVC Add
Write the bash command to add `'model.pkl'` to DVC tracking.

lessons.title: Automated Evaluation of RAG & Agent Performance Metrics
lessons.theory:
## RAG Metrics
RAG systems are often evaluated on context relevancy, faithfulness, and answer correctness.
```python
score = evaluator.evaluate(answer, context)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: RAG Metric
Assign `'Faithfulness'` to the variable describing if an answer is purely derived from the retrieved context.

lessons.title: Deploying Models safely with API Gateways & Gradio
lessons.theory:
## Gradio
A fast way to build UIs for ML models.
```python
import gradio as gr
gr.Interface(fn=predict, inputs="text", outputs="text").launch()
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Launch Interface
Create a Gradio Interface using the function `generate`, input `'image'`, output `'text'`, and call `launch()`.
lessons.solution:
import gradio as gr
gr.Interface(fn=generate, inputs="image", outputs="text").launch()

lessons.title: Monitoring Model Drift & Prompt Leakage in Production
lessons.theory:
## Data Drift
When the statistical properties of the input data change over time, degrading model accuracy.
```python
if drift_score > threshold:
    alert("Retrain model")
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Drift Concept
Assign `'Data Drift'` to the variable representing changing input distributions.

lessons.title: Decentralized Fine-tuning Compute & GPU Orchestration
lessons.theory:
## GPU Orchestration
Tools like Ray distribute Python code and model training across multiple GPUs/nodes.
```python
import ray
ray.init()
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Initialize Ray
Write the code to import `ray` and initialize the cluster connection.
